search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG12 skin cancer lesion referral
candidate_sources_rejected: ABCDE teaching graphics not issued as exact guidance
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Unopened educational graphics were not used as evidence.
Commercial screening material was not authoritative.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for suspicious melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma features; not exact for every benign lesion, skin tag, papule, or surveillance visit.
setting_applicability: Exact for primary-care recognition, safety netting, and referral; partial for dermatology procedures and routine monitoring.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE skin-lesion guideline was identified; NICE criteria and pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The official NICE NG12 recommendations, last updated 2026-04-15, were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: NICE NG12 remains the current official suspected-cancer recognition and referral guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped suspicious-lesion terms, size or colour change, irregular morphology, diameter, inflammation, oozing or sensation context, dermoscopy, and referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped return, warning-symptom, concern, monitoring, and arranged-review documentation only when clinician-discussed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Benign lesions, skin tags, screening, sun and family history, itching or pain, bleeding terminology, ABCDE wording, biopsy reports, procedure choices, sun-protection advice, and fixed three-to-six-month surveillance remain unsupported.
Suspicion, diagnosis, and referral urgency remain clinician-stated and locally determined.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
